name: Quality Tier 3 — 2-table FK joins
description: Two-table inner joins via foreign key. Each scenario carries the expected row count (or first-row scalar) so the run can be graded semantically. Tests join graph resolution + FK label projection + filter on a joined table.

Scenarios.Question: list every ticket with its priority name
Scenarios.ExpectedShape: SELECT Tickets.TicketNumber + Tickets.Title + TicketPriorities.Name, INNER JOIN TicketPriorities, soft-delete filter on Tickets
Scenarios.Notes: FK label-projection check. Every ticket should appear with its priority name.

Scenarios.Question: list outages along with the service type name
Scenarios.ExpectedShape: SELECT Outages.* + ServiceTypes.NameEn/Name, INNER JOIN ServiceTypes
Scenarios.Notes: Different root, same shape as QT3-LIST-TICK-PRIO. Validates the FK-walk is schema-driven not table-specific.

Scenarios.Question: show all tariffs with the service type they apply to
Scenarios.ExpectedShape: SELECT Tariffs.* + ServiceTypes.NameEn, INNER JOIN ServiceTypes
Scenarios.Notes: Smallest 2-table join (5 rows). Easy to eyeball.

Scenarios.Question: how many tickets are critical priority
Scenarios.ExpectedShape: COUNT(*) over Tickets JOIN TicketPriorities WHERE TicketPriorities.Name='Critical' — single-row aggregate, empty select
Scenarios.Notes: Filter is on a JOINED LOOKUP table's Name column (exact match). The classic 'X by lookup-value' shape that should route through the FK-target's label column.

Scenarios.Question: how many outages affected electricity
Scenarios.ExpectedShape: COUNT(*) over Outages JOIN ServiceTypes WHERE ServiceTypes.NameEn LIKE/eq 'Electricity'
Scenarios.Notes: Different root + different lookup table than QT3-CNT-TICKET-CRITICAL. Validates the shape is universal not table-specific.
